headline: WATCH: D.C. officials share updates on the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting
reading:
The suspect in a shooting incident at Saturday's correspondents' dinner attended by President Donald Trump and other senior U.S. officials was carrying guns and knives and was believed to be acting alone, the mayor of Washington said.

Trump said the suspect was armed with multiple weapons before stopped by Secret Service. One officer was shot, but he was protected by a bulletproof vest.

A man armed with guns and knives stormed the lobby outside a high-profile journalists' dinner attended by President Donald Trump and multiple senior U.S. The president was uninjured and was hustled away.

The passage explains recent developments using multiple reports.
The events are connected across different reports.
It combines information from multiple sources.

newsItems.title: WATCH: D.C. officials share updates on the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting
newsItems.content: The suspect in a shooting incident at Saturday's correspondents' dinner attended by President Donald Trump and other senior U.S. officials was carrying guns and knives and was believed to be acting alone, the mayor of Washington said.

newsItems.title: WATCH: Trump holds press conference after shooting at White House Correspondents' Dinner
newsItems.content: Trump said the suspect was armed with multiple weapons before stopped by Secret Service. One officer was shot, but he was protected by a bulletproof vest.

newsItems.title: Trump unharmed after security incident at White House Correspondents' Dinner
newsItems.content: A man armed with guns and knives stormed the lobby outside a high-profile journalists' dinner attended by President Donald Trump and multiple senior U.S. leaders on Saturday night. The president was uninjured and was hustled away.

newsItems.title: One police officer killed and another critically injured in Chicago hospital shooting, officials say
newsItems.content: A police officer died and another was critically injured after a shooting at a Chicago hospital on Saturday, according to officials.

newsItems.title: Mexico says 2 U.S. federal agents who died were not authorized to participate in any local operation
newsItems.content: Mexico's government said Saturday that two U.S. federal agents recently killed in a car crash in the country's northern region were not authorized to participate in operations in Mexico.
